Dear Mr. Hopkins: 
Re: 
Your Request for Wri~ten 
Advice, Our No. A-83-009 
Thank you for your letter requesting our written ~aVlce. 
Your question involves the interpretation of the Levine Bill, 
AB 1040, Chap. 1049, Stats. 1982 (Government Code Section 
84308), and ~~e Commission's recently-approved regulations* 
interpreting that statute. 
Specifically, you aSK whether the Anaheim City Council is 
exempted under Section 843D8(d) when it ac~s in its entirety as 
the City's Redevelopment Agency or as the City's Housing 
Authority. 
That subdivision exempts: 
Legislative oodies such as city councils ... while 
acting as a oody or a committee thereof .... ,However, 
this section [84308] applies to any member of SUCh a 
legislative body who serves as a voting memoer on 
another quaSi-judicial board or commission wnich con-
siders individual applications for licenses, permits, 
or entitlements for use. 
*These regulations have not yet been filed with the 
Secretary of State. 
Although these regulations are not yet 
effective, we are using them as guidelines for interpreting 
Section 84308 whenever we are asked for advice under 
Section 83114. 


William P. Hopkins 
January 21, 1983 
Page Two 
These provisions are interpreted in the proposed 2 Cal. Adm. 
Code Section 18438.1(b) * which provides: 
(b) 
A legislative body is "acting as a body or a 
committee thereof" when: 
(1) 
It acts in its entirety as itself or as 
the ex officio governing body of any distri=t or 
other entity; or 
(2) 
Any subgroup of the legislative body 
composed solely ,of memoers of the cody sics cy 
designation of the body. 
Therefore, when a city council sits as another body, buc does so 
in its entiretY', without any outside members, it is "acting as a 
body or committee thereof" within the meaning of Government Code 
Section 84308(d) and its members are exempt from the other 
provisions of Section 84308 when serving in that capacity. 
So 
long as there are no outside members on the City of &,aheim's 
Redevelopment Agency Board or Housing Authority Board, tne city 
councilmemoers sitting in those capacities are exempt. 
(~or 
further explanation, see our Advice Letter No. A-83-008, to 
Betsy Strauss, City Attorney of Napa, copy enclos~d.) 
Should you have further questions, please do not hesitate to 
call me at 916/322-5901.

Dear Mr. Leidigh: 
~ephon.e: 
7'i3:?99-5169 
(:;)

' 
4 
I 
~ 
~Ti th reference to our telephone conversation of January 
14, 1983, I would appreciate an informal letter from your 
office concerning the FPPC interpretation of the Levine Bill 
(Government Code 84308) with respect to the exemption of a 
City Council from the provisions of the law while acting as a 
body or committee thereof. 
Specifically, our question relates to the exemption of 
a City Council while acting as a Redevelopment Agency and or 
Housing Authority when all members of the City Council also 
act as the Redevelopment Agency and Housing Authority. 
The letter requested would confirm your interpretation 
of Administrative Regulation 18438.1 (a) and (b) exempting 
the City Council members under the above circumstances. 
Thank you for your assistance in this matter. 
HPH/vh

sections.question: Whether the Anaheim City Council is exempted under Section 84308(d) when it acts in its entirety as the City's Redevelopment Agency or as the City's Housing Authority.
sections.conclusion: When a city council sits as another body, but does so in its entirety, without any outside members, it is 'acting as a body or committee thereof' within the meaning of Government Code Section 84308(d) and its members are exempt from the other provisions of Section 84308 when serving in that capacity. So long as there are no outside members on the City of Anaheim's Redevelopment Agency Board or Housing Authority Board, the city council members sitting in those capacities are exempt.
sections.question_synthetic: Whether city council members are exempt from the Levine Bill (Government Code Section 84308) disclosure requirements when the entire council acts as both a Redevelopment Agency and Housing Authority without outside members.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: City council members are exempt from Government Code Section 84308 when the entire council acts as a Redevelopment Agency or Housing Authority provided no outside members are present on those boards. The exemption applies because the council is 'acting as a body or committee thereof' under proposed regulations interpreting the statute.
sections.parsing_notes: No section headers found; LLM: Document contains both the incoming request (from City Attorney dated January 14, 1983) and the FPPC response (dated January 21, 1983). Response letter is clearly structured with explicit question and conclusion sections. Minor OCR errors present but do not significantly impact extraction. Document explicitly references proposed regulations not yet filed with Secretary of State.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Whether the Anaheim City Council is exempted under Section 84308(d) when it acts in its entirety as the City's Redevelopment Agency or as the City's Housing Authority.

CONCLUSION: When a city council sits as another body, but does so in its entirety, without any outside members, it is 'acting as a body or committee thereof' within the meaning of Government Code Section 84308(d) and its members are exempt from the other provisions of Section 84308 when serving in that capacity. So long as there are no outside members on the City of Anaheim's Redevelopment Agency Board or Housing Authority Board, the city council members sitting in those capacities are exempt.

embedding.summary: The FPPC advised the City Attorney of Anaheim that city council members are exempt from the Levine Bill's conflict of interest provisions when the entire council serves as the Redevelopment Agency or Housing Authority without outside members. The exemption is based on the interpretation that such action constitutes the legislative body 'acting as a body or committee thereof' under Government Code Section 84308(d).
